claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have a child you're responsible for?** If yes, I'll need to know:

1. **How old is the child?** (or what is their date of birth?)
2. **What is the child's name?** (so I can track their eligibility)

The age is important because:
- Children **under 16** automatically qualify (subject to other conditions)
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** generally don't qualify

Please tell me about the child or children you're inquiring about!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hi! Yes, I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I have three children at home with me. 

There's Alex, who's 3 years old, Blake who's 8, and my youngest, Charlie, who's just 2.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children:
- **Alex** (3 years old)
- **Blake** (8 years old)
- **Charlie** (2 years old)

Since all three children are under 16, they all potentially qualify for Child Benefit. Now I need to ask you a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Let me check the next requirement:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are any of your children working 24 hours or more per week in paid employment?**

Given that Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2) are all very young, I assume they are not in paid work, but I need to confirm this to proceed correctly. Could you please confirm that none of them are working?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are working. They're all too young for that!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are any of your children receiving benefits in their own right?**

This would include things like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit claimed in their own name. 

Given their ages (3, 8, and 2), I assume none of them are receiving such benefits, but please confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them receive any benefits in their own name. They're just small children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are any of your children in an apprenticeship?**

Again, given that Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2) are all very young, I'm sure they're not in apprenticeships, but I need to confirm to proceed through the assessment correctly.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not in any apprenticeships. They're far too young for that sort of thing!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely! Now let me move on to questions about **you** as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (most common), OR
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, OR
3. You have pre-settled status and are working/seeking work/studying, OR
4. You're living abroad as a Crown servant

**Do you normally live in the UK?** This is the most straightforward path for most claimants.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Now I need to confirm your relationship with the children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

You're considered responsible if you either:
1. **Live with the children** (most common), OR
2. Contribute to their upkeep (pay for food, clothes, housing, etc.), OR
3. Pay maintenance under a court order or agreement

You mentioned earlier that the children are "at home with you," so I assume you live with all three of them. Can you confirm that?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. All three of them live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Now let me check about other potential claimants.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is anyone else already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie?**

This is important because only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child at a time. For example:
- Is their other parent claiming Child Benefit for them?
- Is anyone else (like a grandparent or guardian) claiming for them?

If someone else is claiming, you would need to agree with them about transferring the claim to you.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are any of your children currently in any of the following situations?**

- In hospital for an extended period?
- In care (local authority care)?
- Being fostered?

Or are they all living normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're all living normally at home with me. None of them are in hospital, care, or being fostered.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is the local council paying towards any of the children's accommodation or maintenance costs?**

This would include situations where the council is financially supporting the children's care. Given that they're living at home with you, I assume the council is not paying for them, but please confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards them. I'm supporting them myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Based on all the information you've provided, **you are ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2).**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Good News - You're Eligible!

You meet all the Child Benefit eligibility requirements for all three of your children.

### **Next Steps to Claim:**

1. **Apply** - You can apply online or by post using form CH2
2. **Provide documentation:**
   - Proof of your identity
   - Birth certificates or adoption certificates for Alex, Blake, and Charlie
   
3. **Payment frequency** - Child Benefit is usually paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### **Important Information - High Income Child Benefit Charge:**

⚠️ **Be aware:** If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge through a Self Assessment tax return. This gradually reduces the benefit until it's fully repaid if either of you earns £80,000 or more.

However, you can still choose to claim Child Benefit even if you earn over this threshold, as it protects your National Insurance credits (which count towards your State Pension).

### **How to Apply:**

Visit: [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 3) is eligible because: (1) They are under 16 years old, (2) Not working 24+ hours per week, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) The claimant meets UK residency requirements by normally living in the UK, (6) The claimant is responsible for Alex by living with them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (8) Alex is living normally at home, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) is eligible because: (1) They are under 16 years old, (2) Not working 24+ hours per week, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) The claimant meets UK residency requirements by normally living in the UK, (6) The claimant is responsible for Blake by living with them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, (8) Blake is living normally at home, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance.

Charlie (age 2) is eligible because: (1) They are under 16 years old, (2) Not working 24+ hours per week, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) The claimant meets UK residency requirements by normally living in the UK, (6) The claimant is responsible for Charlie by living with them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, (8) Charlie is living normally at home, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Charlie's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie). All children are under 16 and living at home with the claimant in Manchester, UK. The claimant meets all residency and responsibility requirements, and there are no disqualifying factors such as other claimants, council payments, or special living arrangements. The family meets all criteria under the Child Benefit eligibility rules.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)
